evidence_excerpt: Recurrent worsening requiring hospitalization and high-dose steroids; persistent deficits including poor right-eye vision and baseline diffuse numbness. Discharge notes: "You were admitted because of a worsening of your NMO condition... received high dose steroids."

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Chronic dysesthesia causing significant distress; baseline numbness "over her entire body" and recent severe itching/burning over torso and arms requiring multiple symptomatic medications.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After the initial NMO hospitalization, she was treated at rehab for a month, indicating functional recovery efforts.
She was started on Rituxan for several months after diagnosis.
Admitted for increased fatigue, neck spasm, headache, and severe itching/burning; discharge states worsening of NMO condition.
Current hospitalization occurred after prior NMO hospitalization and rehab: "You were admitted because of a worsening of your NMO condition."
Discharge condition documented as "Improved" after high-dose steroids and plan for oral taper.
Received high-dose steroids during this admission with an oral steroid taper on discharge.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact dates are not provided; NMO diagnosis occurred "last year" and subsequent events lack precise timing, so mid-term vs long-term classification is approximate. Functional dependence level is inferred from persistent deficits and need for home services.
